liveBoundaryPolicy: Live integration and e2e categories run the real Smackerel stack. External Google Drive traffic is represented by an owned fixture boundary while production GoogleDriveProvider code remains in the path.
qualityGates: Each scenario must retain one live regression expectation before a scope can be marked done.
Live categories must execute the real Smackerel stack with no internal request interception or mocked Smackerel service paths.
Evidence references are added only after implementation or validation phases run the planned tests.

scenarios.title: Required drive configuration is generated and fail-loud
scenarios.contract: Drive SST values flow through generated runtime config and missing required values fail startup explicitly.

scenarios.title: Google Drive can connect with scoped access and an empty drive
scenarios.contract: Google connect, access mode, folder scope, empty-drive health, and zero-count UI all succeed through the fixture boundary.

scenarios.title: A second provider registers without downstream branching
scenarios.contract: Two drive providers expose capabilities through the same provider-neutral registry and downstream contract.

scenarios.title: Bulk scan preserves folder hierarchy and provider metadata
scenarios.contract: Initial scan persists one drive identity per provider file, linked artifact identity, folder hierarchy, provider URL, owner, sharing, mime, size, and version metadata.

scenarios.title: Empty drive remains healthy and emits no artifacts
scenarios.contract: Empty initial scan creates no artifact rows, remains healthy, and monitor detects later uploads.

scenarios.title: Provider outage degrades visibly and queues work
scenarios.contract: Provider failures transition health through degraded/failing thresholds while work remains queued or retryable with visible status.

scenarios.title: Multi-format files become searchable and domain-routable
scenarios.contract: PDF, scanned PDF, image, Office, audio, and text fixtures produce indexed content plus classification, sensitivity, evidence, and domain routing metadata.

scenarios.title: Folder move refreshes taxonomy without re-extracting content
scenarios.contract: A folder move with unchanged content revision refreshes folder context and classification without repeating content extraction.

scenarios.title: Blocked and skipped files remain visible with reason and action
scenarios.contract: Size, encrypted, unsupported, permission, and extraction-timeout cases remain visible with identity, folder, reason, action, and health counters.

scenarios.title: Natural-language search returns drive files with context
scenarios.contract: Search results include snippets, provider chips, breadcrumbs, sharing badges, sensitivity badges, and provider links.

scenarios.title: Native Google Doc revisions update one artifact identity
scenarios.contract: Provider revisions keep one current artifact identity while version_chain stores prior revisions visible in the Versions tab.

scenarios.title: Tombstoned or permission-lost files stay explainable
scenarios.contract: Retained metadata/content remains queryable within policy and UI explains tombstone or access-revoked states without offering unavailable bytes.

scenarios.title: Telegram receipt auto-files to Drive
scenarios.contract: A classified Telegram receipt writes through Save Service, records Telegram source and Drive provider URL, and replies with saved location.

scenarios.title: Generated meal plan saves back to Drive
scenarios.contract: Meal-plan save-back writes the expected Drive PDF through the provider fixture and exposes the provider URL to digest.

scenarios.title: Concurrent missing-folder saves create exactly one folder
scenarios.contract: Two simultaneous saves to one missing folder create one provider folder, write both files, and persist one folder resolution mapping.

scenarios.title: Low-confidence classification pauses routing
scenarios.contract: Low-confidence save routing performs no provider write until web or Telegram confirmation resolves exactly once.

scenarios.title: Sensitivity policy blocks unsafe auto-link sharing
scenarios.contract: Medical auto-link sharing is blocked or downgraded with explicit policy reason and no provider share link mutation.

scenarios.title: Overlapping rules audit conflict and execute stable match
scenarios.contract: All matching rules are audited as conflicts while the first stable match executes and Screen 7 surfaces the conflict.

scenarios.title: Telegram retrieves a policy-allowed Drive file
scenarios.contract: Telegram retrieval searches drive artifacts, checks policy, and returns file, provider link, or disambiguation with drive labels.

scenarios.title: Sensitive retrieval never sends bytes over Telegram
scenarios.contract: Sensitive Telegram retrieval sends no raw bytes and returns secure link, provider link, or refusal according to config.

scenarios.title: Drive agent tools enforce contracts and policy
scenarios.contract: drive_search, drive_get_file, drive_save_file, and drive_list_rules enforce authorization, sensitivity policy, neutral identifiers, and traces.

scenarios.title: Drive artifacts feed downstream features without provider branching
scenarios.contract: Recipes, expenses, lists, annotations, meal plan, digest, and agent consumers read provider-neutral artifact metadata, not provider packages.

scenarios.title: Synthetic large-drive workload meets performance and isolation targets
scenarios.contract: The 5,000-file/25 GB synthetic workload meets stress targets and proves disposable-state isolation.

scenarios.title: Multi-provider search returns unified provider-neutral results
scenarios.contract: Google Drive and a second provider return one ranked list with provider, folder, sharing, audience, and sensitivity filters.
